rationale: SB 53 mandates the publication of a Frontier AI Framework. This control establishes the required governance structure to document how the organization identifies and mitigates catastrophic risks aligned with national standards.

Developers must assess whether their models pose catastrophic risks (e.g. CBRN weapons cyberattacks). This control operationalizes the required lifecycle risk assessments specifically for high-impact failure modes.

The law requires the capability to promptly enact a full shutdown (kill switch) of the model. This control governs the technical capacity and decision authority to isolate and terminate AI operations during a loss-of-control event.

Critical safety incidents must be reported to the CA Office of Emergency Services (OES) within 15 days (or 24 hours for imminent threats). This control defines the protocol for timely regulatory notification.
SB 53 grants robust protections to employees reporting safety risks. This control mandates the existence of anonymous

The law requires developers to prevent the exfiltration of unreleased model weights. This control enforces the cryptographic protection and access restrictions necessary to secure these critical intellectual property assets.

Safety frameworks and compliance must be independently verified. This control operationalizes the engagement of third-party auditors to validate the organization's adherence to its published safety commitments.

For organizations deploying frontier models this control requires verifying that the upstream developer has published their required Safety Framework and has not reported critical unmitigated incidents.
